title: Infosys McCamish Systems enters Consent Order with South Dakota Division of Insurance
detailed_summary: Infosys McCamish Systems ('IMS'), a subsidiary of Infosys BPM limited, entered into a Consent Order with the State of South Dakota Division of Insurance on December 22, 2025. The matter related to concerns over potential violations of South Dakota Codified Laws concerning notification with respect to a 2023 data breach and failure to respond to the regulator's request for information within 20 days. IMS resolved the matter without admitting the alleged violations and submitted a $30,000 monetary penalty.

key_facts.fact: On December 22, 2025, Infosys McCamish Systems, a subsidiary of Infosys BPM limited, entered into a Consent Order with the State of South Dakota Division of Insurance.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub: Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: The South Dakota Division of Insurance expressed concerns that IMS may have violated South Dakota Codified Laws related to notification with respect to a 2023 data breach.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub: Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: The Consent Order resolved the matter without any further action and without admission that IMS violated the Acts listed in the document.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub: Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: IMS submitted a payment of $30,000 to the South Dakota Division of Insurance as agreed upon in the consent order as a monetary penalty.
